Indeed, even many highly educated adults have trouble with words vocabulary •  336    abbreviations asap         as soon as possible ‘ before midday’ ) am          in the morning (from Latin ante meridiem, or Aids   AIDS      Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome ‘ in the year of the Lord’ , used before years) AD (AmE A.D.)    after the birth of Christ (from Latin anno domini, see a good dictionary.

many abbreviations commonly used in English. For explanations of others, would have full stops in American English. This is a small selection of the very stops. Some of them could also have full stops in British English, and most The  following abbreviations are given in their common British form, without full 5 some     common       abbreviations but MP's, CD's   more often MPs,  CDs.

An apostrophe (’ ) is sometimes used before the s in the plurals of abbreviations.

not                                                                      not the CIA /,si: aɪ 'eɪ/ (     /^19/) the IRA /<ai ɑ:r 'eɪ/ (     /atrə/) Note that not all initial-letter abbreviations are pronounced as words.

Scientific and Cultural Organisation not UNESCO  /ju:'neskəo/ (     the-UNESCO) = the United Nations Educational, called ‘ acronyms’ . Articles are usually dropped in acronyms.

Some initial-letter abbreviations are pronounced like words. These are often 3 acronyms not the RSPCA /ði: ɑ:r .  .  ./ (     / ðə-oɪr-.  . ./) not - the USA /ðə     ju: .  .  ./ (     /ð  iɪ-juɪ .  .  ./) not an MP  /ən cm  .  .  ./ (     OrMP) - a BA degree not a US diplomat /ə   ju:  .  .  ./ (     an US...) - an EU country abbreviation. Compare: pronunciation of the article depend on the pronunciation of the first letter of the If one of these abbreviations has an article (a/an or the), the form and the BBC/dz ,bi: biɪ 'si:/   the USA /ðə     ,ju: es 'eɪ/ These abbreviations are most often stressed on the last letter.

the BBC = the British Broadcasting Corporation happens with the names of organisations.

Some abbreviations are made from the first letters of several words. This often 2  initial-letter        abbreviations kg (AmE  kg.) = kilogram Ltd = Limited (company) Mr (AmE  Mr.) = Mister (not usually written in full) English. Full stops (AmE periods) are normal in American English.
